Title: Esma Sultan Mansion • Location, Photos and Information About It
URL: https://kulturenvanteri.com/en/yer/esma-sultan-yalisi/
Content: # Esma Sultan Mansion. It is also known as the Baştımar Mansion. Located next to the Büyük Mecidiye Mosque in Ortaköy, the waterside mansion was built by Sarkis Balyan for Sultan Abdülaziz's daughter Esma Sultan between 1873 and 1875. After Esma Sultan's death, the mansion was passed on to Cemile Sultan, the sister of Abdulhamid II, and later to Fatma Sultan. While it was used as a tobacco warehouse, it was also known as the Baştımar Mansion because it was purchased by Saffet Baştımar in 1952. All the ruins of the mansion, whose last owner was Oğuz Gürel, have been preserved, its surroundings have been planted with flowers to give it the air of an archaeological park, and the two-storey wooden outbuilding from its first construction has been restored and furnished in the style of a traditional Anatolian house. Content: ## The perfect wedding day at the Esma Sultan Mansion in Istanbul. Esma Sultan Mansion is spectacularly located right on the Bosphorus, but that's exactly where the challenge lies: the Ortaköy district is one of the most popular and busiest areas of Istanbul - especially at weekends. If you're thinking about organising an entire wedding weekend in Istanbul, the question quickly arises: can you use Esma Sultan Mansion for several days or does it make more sense to focus on the wedding day and plan around it? This means that you can make ideal use of Esma Sultan if you concentrate on the wedding itself and pack the full drama - sunset, water, light, party - into a single, intensive programme.
